samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal.</input>
        <input id="sign">Signed or unsigned operation.</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVIDEND.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVISOR.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">Valid operation request.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">Ready to receive the 16-bit output.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">Valid result output.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">Output result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="IDLE">0</param>
        <param id="SUB">1</param>
        <param id="SEL">2</param>
        <param id="SHIFT">3</param>
        <param id="DONE">4</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="res_valid" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>res_valid is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.</description>
            <condition>
                <description>Update the res_valid at posedge clk or negedge rst.</description>
                <state id="IDLE"/>
                <state id="DONE"/>
                <assert>
                    <description>At rst, the res_valid should be 0.</description>
                    <condition>
                        <description>rst is high.</description>
                        <true>
                            <description>The res_valid should be 0.</description>
                            <false>
                                <description>The res_valid is not 0.</description>
                            </false>
                        </true>
                    </condition>
                </assert>
                <assert>
                    <description>At posedge clk, the res_valid is updated.</description>
                    <condition>
                        <description>At the posedge clk, the res_valid is updated.</description>
                        <true>
                            <description>When cnt is 8, the res_ready is 1.</description>
                            <false>
                                <description>The res_ready is not 1.</description>
                            </false>
                        </true>
                    </condition>
                </assert>
                <assert>
                    <description>At posedge clk, the res_valid is not updated.</description>
                    <condition>
                        <description>At the posedge clk, the res_valid is not updated.</description>
                        <true>
                            <description>The res_valid is not 0.</description>
                            <false>
                                <description>The res_valid is 0.</description>
                            </false>
                        </true>
                    </condition>
                </assert>
            </condition>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.</description>
        <description>Initializes the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.</description>
        <description>Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor.</description>
        <description>Set the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.</description>
        <description>start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps.</description>
        <description>If the counter cnt reaches 8, the division is complete.</description>
        <description>cnt and start_cnt are cleared.</description>
        <description>Updates the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient.</description>
        <description>Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result.</description>
        <description>Computes the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR.</description>
        <description>Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out.</description>
        <description>Updates SR by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.</description>
    </implementation>
</module>
